concept: Ancient / From Long Ago / Deeply Old
description: Belonging to the very distant past; having existed for a very long time. 'Tholua' names the quality of deep time—smonaes older than memory, traditions from before history, the long perspective that dwarfs human lifespans.
sound_symbolism: The word reaches into deep time. 'Tho' /θo/ begins with breath from ages past, time beyond counting. Then 'lu' stretches long, duration extended. Finally 'a' opens to vastness, ages upon ages.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'tholua shumu' (ancient moss). Deeper than 'serao' (old)—implies ages, eras, geological or cosmic time scales.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Ancient knowledge carries tested wisdom—practices refined over generations, understanding earned by deep time.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Contemplating the ancient develops perspective—our concerns shrink against the backdrop of deep time.
